beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the porch, check the weather forecast
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning ritual—Lucia starts her day grounded at home, taking in the overcast but mild weather before deciding how to structure her day. Low energy (40/100) from yesterday means a gentler morning.

beats.activity: Run through Wallingford neighborhood, 5-6 miles at easy pace
beats.reasoning: Fitness obsession drives an early run; overcast weather is perfect for this. Stays local to conserve energy and enjoy the familiar neighborhood on a Saturday.
Post-run recovery and refuel at home; no time wasted on errands this early.

Lucia heads to Ballard (close to her workplace) to browse the Saturday farmers market—fits her fitness-conscious eating, local values, and desire for a low-key Saturday outing. Generous with money means she'll shop freely.

beats.activity: Shop and browse at Ballard Farmers Market, chat with vendors, pick up produce and local items
beats.reasoning: Farmers market is her speed—outdoor, community-focused, supports local. She'll linger, sample items, chat with vendors. The overcast weather makes lingering comfortable.

beats.activity: Lunch at a casual spot in Ballard, grab fresh food from market finds or a nearby cafe
beats.reasoning: Close to the market, good local spot. Lucia stays in Ballard rather than traveling far; she's building momentum for a relaxed day.

beats.activity: Commute to Seattle Athletic Club downtown, gym session
beats.reasoning: Travel time to downtown gym where she can push through a solid strength session. Fitness obsession means a second workout on a lower-energy day—lighter than usual but still intentional.

beats.activity: Weights and cardio session, stretch, shower
beats.reasoning: Solid 1h45min gym block—strength work, some cardio, recovery focus. Overcast day means no FOMO about missing sunshine.
Head back to Wallingford mid-afternoon. Low energy suggests a quieter evening ahead rather than chasing the Mariners game downtown.

beats.activity: Tend to home brewing setup, organize equipment, prepare for next batch; Write letters by hand, settle in with tea, light video gaming (Stardew Valley)
beats.reasoning: Lucia spends time on her home brewing hobby—meditative, hands-on, fits her Saturday pacing. No big social crowds; she prefers quiet focus.
Eat at home with ingredients from the farmers market. Stay grounded and prepare for the week ahead.

world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
